name: Pumpkin Risotto
recipeIngredient: 3 cups pumpkin flesh, peeled and cubed
5 cups chicken stock or vegetable stock
2 cups arborio rice (Japanese rice also works)
1 cup dry white wine (or water or stock)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Wrap the cubed pumpkin in foil and bake for 30 minutes.
2. While the pumpkin is baking, sauté the shallots in a little white wine, water, or stock until they are soft.
3. In a separate pan, bring the chicken stock or vegetable stock to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
4. Puree the baked pumpkin and set aside.
5. Add the rice to the sautéed shallots and cook for 2-3 minutes over moderate heat, stirring frequently.
6. Add the white wine (or water or stock) and powdered saffron, cooking and stirring constantly until all the liquid has been absorbed.
7. Gradually add the pumpkin puree and continue cooking until it is absorbed.
8. Add the chicken stock or vegetable stock 1 cup at a time, stirring constantly until each addition is absorbed before adding more stock. Continue this process until the rice is cooked (about 15-20 minutes total).
9. If you run out of stock, add hot water to maintain the consistency.
10. Remove from heat and stir in the fresh sage, salt, and pepper to taste. Optionally, garnish with grated Parmesan cheese for added flavor.
Serve the risotto warm with white wine and crusty Italian bread on the side.
